core_problem.headline: The 'Bleeding Neck' Problem
core_problem.description: Trade businesses lose significant revenue through missed calls. A missed call is not just a missed connection—it's lost immediate revenue and lifetime value. SMBs miss 20-62% of inbound calls, with weekend/after-hours rates reaching 60%.

missed_call_value.description: Revenue per missed call varies by trade and urgency. Emergency calls command 1.5-2x premium rates.

voicemail_abandonment.description: Callers who reach voicemail typically hang up and dial a competitor immediately.

speed_to_lead.description: Leads contacted within 5 minutes are significantly more likely to convert. Average response time for many businesses is hours or days.

competitor_pivot.description: Customers will switch to a competitor after a poor experience (unanswered call). In high-urgency trades, lack of immediate response = lost deal.

competitive_landscape.limitations: 9-5 only; sick days; training overhead; no after-hours coverage (misses 40% of calls)
Per-minute/per-call billing leads to unpredictable bills; agents lack trade-specific knowledge
None — infinite concurrency, 24/7, trade-trained, $0.25-$0.50/interaction vs $3-6 for humans

web_chat_economics.description: Homeowners prefer texting for project details over phone calls. Millennial homeowners increasingly digital-first.
web_chat_economics.zero_click_lead: Visitors see no instant way to communicate (only phone number) and bounce. AI Web Chat captures passive visitors into active leads.

annual_loss_estimates.assumption: Based on 20% missed call rate × avg ticket × non-callback rate
Higher call volume, similar miss rate patterns
Peak season overflow, after-hours emergency premium

market_context.value_framing: The cost of our solution is less than 1% of the annual revenue loss from missed calls.

pricing_strategy.approach: Door-in-the-Face technique: Present high anchor ($500), retreat to target ($250). The retreat triggers reciprocity—buyer feels they 'won' the negotiation.

packages.name: Growth Bundle
packages.label: Growth Bundle (Anchor)
packages.includes: AI Voice Agent + Website Chat Widget

packages.name: Walk-Away Floor
packages.label: Walk-Away Floor
packages.includes: Minimum viable offer—do not go below

components.name: Base Commission
components.structure: 30% uncapped recurring on gross collected revenue
components.rationale: Recurring commission aligns rep with retention, not just closes

components.name: Launch Bonus
components.rationale: Accelerates early traction and validates product-market fit

components.name: Minimum Guarantee
components.rationale: Protects rep from early churn outside their control (even if client cancels)

components.name: Resignation Tail
components.rationale: Applies to voluntary departure or termination without cause; no production requirement during tail

components.name: Expansion Revenue
components.structure: Clients exclusively attributed for all future upgrades
components.rationale: Incentivizes upsell conversations and relationship maintenance

components.name: Activity Requirement
components.rationale: Ensures active engagement without punitive quotas

components.name: Payment Terms
components.structure: Issued within 7 business days of client payment clearing
components.rationale: Fast, predictable cash flow for contractors

components.name: Clawback Policy
components.structure: Proportional clawback on refunds within 60 days only
components.rationale: Limited exposure; refunds after 60 days don't affect commission

cold_call.goal: Book a 10-minute demo call by agitating the pain of missed emergency revenue

segments.label: Opener (Pattern Interrupt)
segments.script: Hi [Name], this is [Rep]. I'm not calling to sell you leads or SEO. I'm calling because I see you're one of the top [Trade] in [City], and I wanted to ask—how are you handling the emergency calls you miss when you're asleep or on a job site?

segments.label: Bridge (Pain Agitation)
segments.script: We ran a study that showed most local contractors miss about 30% of their calls, mostly nights and weekends. For a business your size, that's usually about $5,000 to $10,000 a month walking out the door to your competitors. Does that sound about right?

segments.label: Solution (Employee Reframe)
segments.script: I have an AI system that acts like a 24/7 receptionist. It answers every call, filters out the spam, takes the job details, and books the appointment directly on your calendar. It sounds human, it never sleeps, and it costs less than your phone bill.

segments.label: Anchor Pitch ($500)
segments.script: Normally, for the full system that handles your phone lines and puts a live agent on your website too, we charge $500 a month. It's a full office staff replacement. Is capturing those extra 10-15 jobs a month worth a 10-minute conversation?

segments.label: Retreat ($250)
segments.script: I get it. $500 is a commitment if you haven't seen it work yet. Tell you what—let's strip off the website stuff for now. Let's just protect your phone line. I can deploy just the Voice Agent for $250 a month. That's $8 a day to never miss a job. If it catches one call, it pays for itself. Fair?

plumbers_nightmare.goal: Alternate opener specifically for plumbing businesses - emphasizes 3 AM emergencies

segments.label: Opener
segments.script: Hi, is this [Owner Name]? This is [Rep]. I'm calling because I saw you guys are open 24/7 on Google, but I know how hard it is to actually answer the phone at 3 AM. We've got a new AI that handles those midnight emergency calls so you can sleep, and it only wakes you up if it's a real paying job. Is that worth a 2-minute chat?

plumbers_nightmare.why_it_works: Acknowledges their hard work ('I know how hard it is'), validates their offer ('open 24/7'), and offers immediate relief ('so you can sleep').

reciprocity_negotiation.goal: Use when prospect rejects the $500 bundle - leverage reciprocity principle

segments.label: Price Retreat
segments.script: I understand. $500 is a lot to add to overhead right now, especially if you're watching margins. Look, I really want you to see the power of not missing calls. What if I drop the website chat for now? I can give you the 24/7 Voice Agent—which is the most important part for catching those emergency calls—for just $250 a month. That's $8 a day to never miss a job. Can we start there?

reciprocity_negotiation.why_it_works: Concession ('I really want you to see...'), Reframing ($250 → $8/day), and Contrast ($500 → $250).

objections.response: I agree—people hate voicemail menus. But they hate waiting even more. Data shows 62% of consumers prefer a bot over waiting. Would you rather they talk to my AI instantly, or your competitor's voicemail?
That's great for 9-to-5. But what about 2 AM when a pipe bursts? Or when she's at lunch? This doesn't replace them; it's their backup. It catches the overflow so you never miss a dime.

Let's do the math. What's your average ticket? $300? If this system saves you one missed call a month, it's free. Everything after that is pure profit. Can you honestly say you don't miss one call a month?

objections.trigger: Is this legal? AI calls are illegal
objections.response: The FCC cracked down on spam outbound cold calling. This is for inbound calls—people calling YOU. It's 100% legal to answer your own phone with an assistant. We also include a clear disclosure so your customers know they're being helped instantly.
Our system is trained specifically on [Trade]. It knows what a 'flapper' is, it knows what a 'capacitor' is. It's not generic ChatGPT; it's a trade-specific agent built for your industry.

compliance.title: AI Disclosure (Required)
compliance.content: The AI must identify itself: 'Hi, I am an automated assistant for [Company Name].' This satisfies transparency requirements and emerging state laws.

compliance.title: Inbound Safe Harbor
compliance.content: FCC/TCPA restrictions target unsolicited outbound calls. Inbound calls (customer-initiated) are generally exempt from strict consent requirements.

compliance.title: Recording Disclosure
compliance.content: Two-party consent states (CA, FL, etc.) require: 'This call may be recorded for quality purposes' immediately upon connection.

compliance.title: Data Security
compliance.content: Customer addresses and payment info require enterprise-grade encryption. Emphasize 'Compliance-First' positioning vs. cheap non-compliant tools.
